baseline_dominance.justification: Construction claims 6 structural wins over RFC 8693 + BBS+ but rfc7662_advocate Attack 1 demonstrates an AS with per-RS introspection policy can return a signed, filtered audit assertion (chain_valid, monotonic_narrowing, final_scope) hiding intermediate scopes and participants — achieving the core claimed property with zero ZK machinery. The construction never explicitly names the AS as adversarial or proves why AS-attested audit is insufficient. Attack 2 shows PPIDs + DPoP already break cross-hop participant linkability at the RS level. The construction is preferable in the narrow 'AS is adversarial or offline' scenario but the baseline approximates the headline property with standard configuration.

formal_security_argument.justification: Four named security games (narrowing forgery, participant extraction, scope extraction, length extraction) with reductions to PLONK soundness + Poseidon collision resistance — this is a real threat model with named assumptions. However, cryptographer Attack 1 shows the length extraction game lacks a formal indistinguishability argument for dummy-hop padding (reduction to PLONK soundness does not cover distinguishability of padding — requires a separate ZK simulatability argument). Attack 2 identifies a semantic gap where empty-scope bypass (scope=0) satisfies the narrowing predicate trivially, meaning the narrowing forgery game definition is under-specified without a liveness predicate. Attack 3 reveals the PLONK trusted setup assumption is not surfaced as a named assumption despite being load-bearing for the journalist scenario. Attack 4 shows participant extraction is defined single-proof only, not over an adaptive multi-proof adversary — breaking the whistleblower claim. Threat model exists but multiple reductions are incomplete or missing.

implementability.justification: Construction explicitly maps to existing Bolyra primitives: Poseidon2/4 for scope commitments, EdDSA on BabyJubjub for delegation signatures, BinaryMerkleRoot for chain anchoring, PLONK for the proof system. Circuit cost is stated at ~110K constraints with <5s PLONK proving time. These are within Phase 1 budget ranges for PLONK circuits. No new primitives required.

adversarial_survival.justification: All five personas found attacks explicitly outside the construction's threat model that the construction cannot answer. Cryptographer: empty-scope bypass renders narrowing vacuous (Attack 2), multi-proof correlation breaks participant privacy for the journalist scenario (Attack 4). cu_ciso: NCUA Part 748 requires named entities in audit logs — the ZK hiding property structurally violates this (Attack 1); no trapdoor reveal or regulatory escrow mechanism exists for incident response (Attack 2); no key revocation primitive (Attack 3). auth0_pm: <5s per proof is catastrophic for sub-100ms AI pipeline hops (Attack 1); 8-hop ceiling has no migration path (Attack 2); auditor model incompatible with NCUA examination procedures (Attack 3). spiffe_engineer: chain completeness unverifiable beyond 8 hops (Attack 1); scope commitments unbound to runtime policy evaluation (Attack 2). rfc7662_advocate: AS-filtered introspection achieves the headline property without ZK (Attack 1). The overwhelming majority of attacks are marked 'not in threat model' and the construction has no mechanism to address them.

scenario_fit.justification: NCUA/SECU loan pipeline and journalist/source scenarios are concretely named with regulatory stakeholders. However, cu_ciso Attacks 1-2 demonstrate the NCUA scenario is structurally incompatible: NCUA examination procedures require legible, reconstructible audit trails with named third-party entities — the construction's privacy guarantee (hiding all intermediate participants) would cause a regulatory examination failure, not pass one. The construction lacks a RegulatorReveal mode or witness escrow. The journalist/source scenario is concrete but niche and undermined by cryptographer Attack 4 (multi-proof correlation) and spiffe_engineer Attack 3 (trust domain membership leakage). Scenarios are named but the construction's properties are misaligned with what those scenarios actually require.

gaps: Construction must explicitly name the AS as adversarial and prove why AS-attested filtered introspection is insufficient — or the baseline dominance claim collapses
Add formal indistinguishability argument for dummy-hop padding in the length extraction game (separate from PLONK soundness reduction)
Add in-circuit liveness predicate (scope[i] != 0 for non-dummy hops) to close the empty-scope bypass
Surface PLONK trusted setup as a named assumption; for journalist scenario either use transparent setup (IPA/STARK) or formalize the trust assumption
Define participant extraction game over adaptive multi-proof adversary to support the whistleblower/journalist claim
Add RegulatorReveal mode: encrypted witness escrow that lets a credentialed NCUA examiner reconstruct the full chain post-incident while hiding it during routine audits
Address chain completeness: auditor must be able to verify the proof covers the entire chain, not just the first 8 of N hops
Define how scope commitments are bound to runtime authorization decisions (not just prover-supplied) for the AI pipeline scenario
Specify verifier integration surface (REST endpoint, WASM library, hosted oracle) and trust bootstrapping for auditor adoption
Add key epoch mechanism with revocation to handle root key compromise without invalidating all historical proofs
